Foreword
Sustainable agricultural productivity, food and nutrition security and poverty 
reduction remain top goals of governments and development institutions 
around the world. Yet, progress is under threat from a variety of crises, 
including climate change and public health emergencies and their associated 
economic and environmental shocks. The transformation to more sustainable, secure and equable agrifood 
systems needs investments in agriculture, rural infrastructure, natural  
resource management and climate resilience. However, agricultural invest-
ments often prioritize the physical or natural capital of farming communities. Investing in farmers’ education, knowledge, habits, experiences and 
attributes – or agriculture human capital – is crucial to drive innovation, boost 
productivity, strengthen farm management and empower smallholders. Building agriculture human capital is fundamental to developing equable, 
secure, resilient and sustainable farming communities. It is key to successful 
agriculture and rural development policies. Beginning in early 2020, the FAO Investment Centre partnered with the 
International Food Policy Research Institute (IFPRI), with support from the 
CGIAR Research Program on Policies, Institutions, and Markets (PIM) and the 
FAO Research and Extension Unit, to examine agriculture human capital 
investments globally. The goal was to understand how farmers developed 
their human capital through a variety of initiatives. The study shows that investments in developing the human capital of 
smallholder producers resulted in new technical and business skills and 
empowered farmers. This led to increased incomes, improved yields and the 
inclusion of marginalised groups. As global agrifood systems face ongoing disruptions, challenges and 
opportunities, agriculture human capital must keep pace. We need more  
and better investments in innovative and cost-effective programmes to 
strengthen and measure human capital development. This toolkit supports investors – including policymakers, government 
officials, international and national development banks and the private sector 
–  to make sounder investment decisions on projects, programmes, and 
policies to strengthen farmers’ capacities. We believe this toolkit makes a unique contribution to the agriculture 
investment landscape. We invite you to use the evidence, strategies, good 
practices, guidance and recommendations it contains on how to best invest 
in farmers.

SERIES: INVESTMENT-TOOLKITS NO5  
TITLE: HOW TO INVEST IN FARMERS? A GUIDE FOR AGRICULTURE HUMAN CAPITAL INVESTMENT PROJECTS  
AUTHORS: JOHANNA GAMMELGAARD(IFPRI-CONSULTANT) ∧ STEVEN FRANZEL(IFPRI-CONSULTANT) ∧ RODRIGO SALCEDO DU BOIS(FAO)  
AUTHORS CONT: ASHOK KUMAR(TRANSFORMING-RURAL-INDIA-FOUNDATION) ∧ KRISTIN DAVIS(IFPRI) ∧ JOHN PREISSING(FAO) ∧ KATARZYNA PANKOWSKA(FAO)  
PUB: FOOD-AND-AGRICULTURE-ORGANIZATION-UNITED-NATIONS ∧ INTERNATIONAL-FOOD-POLICY-RESEARCH-INSTITUTE  
LOCATION: ROME  
DATE:2023  

CONTENTS: FOREWORD; ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS; ABBREVIATIONS; INTRODUCTION; MODULES1–7; REFERENCES; GLOSSARY; ANNEX; TABLES; FIGURES; BOXES  

H1: FOREWORD  
CLAIM: SUSTAINABLE-AGRICULTURAL-PRODUCTIVITY ∧ FOOD-AND-NUTRITION-SECURITY ∧ POVERTY-REDUCTION REMAIN TOP GOALS FOR GOVERNMENTS ∧ DEVELOPMENT-INSTITUTIONS GLOBALLY.  
CLAIM: PROGRESS THREATENED BY CLIMATE-CHANGE ∧ PUBLIC-HEALTH-EMERGENCIES ∧ ECONOMIC ∧ ENVIRONMENTAL-SHOCKS.  
CLAIM: TRANSFORMATION→SUSTAINABLE∧SECURE∧EQUABLE-AGRIFOOD-SYSTEMS NEED INVESTMENTS IN AGRICULTURE ∧ RURAL-INFRASTRUCTURE ∧ NATURAL-RESOURCE-MANAGEMENT ∧ CLIMATE-RESILIENCE.  
CLAIM: AGRICULTURAL-INVESTMENTS OFTEN PRIORITIZE PHYSICAL/NATURAL-CAPITAL OF FARMING-COMMUNITIES.  
CLAIM: INVESTING IN FARMERS’ EDUCATION∧KNOWLEDGE∧HABITS∧EXPERIENCES∧ATTRIBUTES OR AGRICULTURE-HUMAN-CAPITAL IS CRUCIAL TO DRIVE INNOVATION ∧ BOOST PRODUCTIVITY ∧ STRENGTHEN FARM-MANAGEMENT ∧ EMPOWER SMALLHOLDERS.  
CLAIM: BUILDING AGRICULTURE-HUMAN-CAPITAL IS FUNDAMENTAL TO DEVELOPING EQUABLE∧SECURE∧RESILIENT∧SUSTAINABLE FARMING-COMMUNITIES.  
CLAIM: IT IS KEY TO SUCCESSFUL AGRICULTURE ∧ RURAL-DEVELOPMENT POLICIES.  
DATE:2020-EARLY CTX: PARTNERSHIP INITIATION  
CLAIM: FAO-INVESTMENT-CENTRE PARTNERS WITH IFPRI WITH SUPPORT FROM CGIAR-PIM ∧ FAO-RESEARCH-AND-EXTENSION-UNIT TO EXAMINE GLOBAL AGRICULTURE-HUMAN-CAPITAL-INVESTMENTS.  
CLAIM: GOAL=UNDERSTAND HOW FARMERS DEVELOP HUMAN-CAPITAL VIA VARIOUS INITIATIVES.  
RESULT: SMALLHOLDER-HUMAN-CAPITAL-INVESTMENTS → NEW TECHNICAL ∧ BUSINESS-SKILLS ∧ FARMER-EMPOWERMENT.  
RESULT: FARMER-EMPOWERMENT → INCOME↑ ∧ YIELD↑ ∧ INCLUSION OF MARGINALIZED-GROUPS.  
CLAIM: GLOBAL-AGRIFOOD-SYSTEMS FACING DISRUPTIONS∧CHALLENGES∧OPPORTUNITIES; AGRICULTURE-HUMAN-CAPITAL MUST KEEP PACE.  
CLAIM: NEED INVESTMENTS IN INNOVATIVE ∧ COST-EFFECTIVE PROGRAMMES TO STRENGTHEN ∧ MEASURE HUMAN-CAPITAL-DEVELOPMENT.  
CLAIM: TOOLKIT SUPPORTS INVESTORS INCLUDING POLICYMAKERS ∧ GOVT-OFFICIALS ∧ DEVELOPMENT-BANKS ∧ PRIVATE-SECTOR TO MAKE SOUND INVESTMENT-DECISIONS ON PROJECTS∧PROGRAMMES∧POLICIES THAT STRENGTHEN FARMERS’ CAPACITIES.  
CLAIM: TOOLKIT MAKES UNIQUE CONTRIBUTION TO AGRICULTURE-INVESTMENT-LANDSCAPE.  
CALL: INVITE USERS TO USE EVIDENCE∧STRATEGIES∧GOOD-PRACTICES∧GUIDANCE∧RECOMMENDATIONS FOR BEST FARMER-INVESTMENTS.
